commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

commit.message:
perf: major query optimization for judgment browsing

Database optimizations (on VPS):
- Run ANALYZE to populate sqlite_stat1 (enables smart index selection)
- Create 3-column composite indexes:
  - idx_j_source_court_date(source, court_body, judgment_date DESC)
  - idx_j_source_year_date(source, year_hijri, judgment_date DESC)
- Drop 6 redundant/duplicate indexes
- WAL checkpoint (79MB WAL → clean)

Code changes:
- Change all court_body LIKE '%...%' to exact match = (enables index usage)
- Same for committee_ar LIKE on CRSD decisions
- Increase cache_size: 64MB → 128MB, mmap_size: 256MB → 1GB
- Clean up db.ts index definitions (remove redundant ones)
- Fix بقرارمجلس → بقرار مجلس typo in regulation #4

Results: court-filtered query 3.14s → 12ms (261x faster)

feat: refonte cron notification email + route désabonnement + documentation

Cron notification_task.php (refonte complète) :
- Envoi en mode queue phpBB (messenger(true)) pour compatibilité avec le
  système de delivery phpBB sans blocage SMTP synchrone.
- Budget d'exécution 55s, max 50 auteurs par run.
- Token de désabonnement HMAC signé avec cookie_seed.
- Gestion propre des erreurs avec try-catch + update last_run garanti.
- Nettoyage des orphelins en début de run.

Route désabonnement :
- config/routing.yml : ajout de la route /reactions/unsubscribe (GET).

Templates email :
- reaction_digest.txt : template texte phpBB messenger (utilisé réellement).
- reaction_digest.html : template HTML de référence visuelle (non utilisé par
  le messenger qui n'accepte que .txt).

Langues :
- language/en/email.php + language/fr/email.php : clés L_REACTION_FROM,
  L_ON_DATE, L_VIEW_POST, L_UNSUBSCRIBE_LINK pour les templates.

Documentation :
- README.fr.md : guide complet incluant la section crontab système recommandée
  pour la résilience en production (watchdog + backup cron reactions).
- README.md : mise à jour documentation EN.

Context : locks cron phpBB orphelins répétés (2026-03-06), corrigés dans
adminhelper par surcharge de cron.event_listener avec try-finally.

commit.message:
fix: use service client for writes in all API routes to prevent RLS staleness

Session-scoped createClient() JWT can go stale during slow LLM/scraping
work, causing RLS to silently reject writes. Switches all .insert/.update/
.delete calls to createServiceClient() across 10 routes, matching the
pattern already proven in /api/ingest. Reads remain on session client.

commit.message:
Lazy-load non-critical JS with requestIdleCallback to reduce TBT

Move to requestIdleCallback (browser idle time, does not count toward TBT):
- All analytics/tracking event listeners
- Dashboard keyboard shortcut
- Lightbox setup
- Product hover (Hot Lead) tracking
- Scroll depth & section tracking
- Social/WhatsApp tracking

Keep in critical DOMContentLoaded path (essential UI only):
- GCLID capture
- Navbar toggle & close
- Form dynamic fields (cargo options, quantity)
- Form submit + Google Sheets fetch

Result: 12.7KB -> 9.9KB minified, fewer blocking tasks on main thread.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ingestion): backfill judge/motion/outcome from existing ruling text (#244) (#248)

Add extract_judge_name() to extract.py using patterns from all CA court
scrapers (LA, SB, SF, Riverside, OC) to recover judge names from stored
ruling text.

Create scripts/backfill_ruling_fields.py that:
- Queries rulings with ruling_text but null judge_id/motion_type/outcome
- Extracts outcome, motion_type, and judge name from ruling text
- Resolves judge names to canonical judge records via resolve_judge()
- Updates ruling rows in batches (configurable size/limit)
- Supports --dry-run mode for safe testing
- Is idempotent (only fills NULL fields, safe to run multiple times)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: diagnostic endpoint uses SUPABASE_URL to match generate-flatlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: fix O(n²) cjson append, ws_broadcast mutex hold, http_dl realloc storm

third_party/cjson.c — attach_child O(n) → O(1):
  Old code walked the full child linked-list to find the tail on every
  cJSON_AddItemToArray / cJSON_AddItemToObject call.  For a 5 000-channel
  IPTV cache this was O(n²): 12.5 M pointer chases per save.
  Fix: store the tail in first_child->prev (unused for non-first nodes).
  cJSON_Delete / GetArraySize / GetArrayItem only traverse ->next, so
  repurposing ->prev is safe.  Reduces 12.5 M chases to 5 000.

core/src/ws.c — ws_broadcast: release mutex before writev():
  Previous code held g_clients_mu for the duration of all writev() calls
  (one per connected client).  This blocked ws_accept() and close_client()
  for as long as the writes took.  Fix: snapshot the fd list under lock,
  release, then iterate and send.  A fd that closes between snapshot and
  send produces EPIPE which writev() drops silently (MSG_NOSIGNAL).

core/src/http_dl.c — exponential buffer growth + connect timeout:
  write_cb called realloc() on every curl chunk (~128× for a 2 MB M3U),
  each potentially triggering malloc + memcpy of the full buffer so far.
  Fix: double the capacity on each growth; ~7 reallocs instead of ~128.
  Also added CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT=10 s to both http_dl_string and
  http_dl_file so an unreachable server fails in 10 s, not 30 s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(security/trivy): add TrivyScanner (#51)

* chore: set kiln scope for graph-security-trivy

* feat(security/trivy): add TrivyScanner with scan and parse_output

Introduces the security sub-package and TrivyScanner, which wraps the
trivy CLI to surface CVE findings as FindingBeads.  scan() invokes
trivy fs . --format json and returns an empty list on FileNotFoundError
or CalledProcessError so callers need no special-case handling when
trivy is absent.  parse_output() maps each Vulnerability entry to a
FindingBead with staleness_class='dependency', lowercased severity, and
an evidence_chain of CVE ID, affected package/version, and fixed
version.

Closes #46

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden CI workflows against prompt injection and supply chain attacks (#4034)

Apply security hardening to GitHub Actions workflows based on an audit
informed by the Clinejection and hackerbot-claw attack patterns:

- claude.yml: Add author_association checks to block untrusted users from
  invoking the AI agent, and restrict allowed_tools to prevent arbitrary
  shell execution via prompt injection
- issue-triage.yml: Remove Bash tool access (replaced with MCP GitHub
  tool for label listing), add prompt injection defense instruction
- CODEOWNERS: Protect CLAUDE.md, .claude/ skills, agents, and rules
  from unauthorized modification (poisoned system prompt vector)
- security-scan.yml: Pin codeql-action and govulncheck-action to SHA
  hashes (were using unpinned tag references)
- releaser.yml: Disable Go module cache for release builds to prevent
  cache poisoning attacks
- pr-size-labeler.yml: Move expression interpolation to env variable
  to prevent injection in github-script context
- image-build-and-publish.yml: Reduce permissions from contents:write
  to contents:read (no git write operations are performed)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Cap pipeline FPS at 30 — IPTV streams report false 60fps

Both r_frame_rate and avg_frame_rate report 59.94 for interlaced
IPTV content that is actually 29.97fps. No cable/IPTV channel
broadcasts true 60fps progressive, so cap at 30 to prevent
double processing overhead.

commit.message:
Phase 1+2: Vite migration, cost optimization, PWA, multi-user auth & sync

Phase 1 — Production Optimization:
- Migrate from Babel Standalone to Vite build (62KB gzipped)
- Model tiering: Haiku for home feed/reviews/details, Sonnet for chat
- Prompt caching with cache_control ephemeral headers
- KV-backed persistent image cache (7-day TTL)
- PWA manifest + service worker for offline/installability
- Reduced home feed frequency (30s→15min, 3→10 searches)

Phase 2 — Multi-User Identity & Server-Side Persistence:
- Cloudflare D1 database with 7 tables (users, products, purchases, etc.)
- PBKDF2 password hashing (100K iterations, SHA-256)
- 6 new API endpoints: register, login, logout, profile update, sync push/pull
- Client sync engine (src/sync.ts) with 3s debounced push
- Onboarding redesign: password fields + login/register toggle
- Background sync on all state mutations
- Cloud Sync status indicator in Settings
- Auth rate limiting (5/min per IP)

commit.message:
Initial release: Pinata + ERC-8004 agent toolkit

TypeScript toolkit for building trustless AI agents with Pinata IPFS storage
and ERC-8004 on-chain identity. Includes:

- AgentStorage: Pinata SDK wrapper for IPFS uploads and retrieval
- AgentRegistry: ERC-8004 on-chain registration, update, and discovery
- AgentCardBuilder: Fluent API for building agent cards
- CLI: register, verify, discover, upload commands
- Multi-network support: Ethereum, Base (mainnet + testnet)
- Read-only discovery mode (no wallet required)
- Example agent cards and scripts

Built by Raidguild Dark Factory.

commit.message:
Remove @supabase/ssr from middleware to fix middleware.js.nft.json error

The @supabase/ssr package imports jose (JWT library) which uses dynamic
code evaluation that breaks Next.js's Node File Trace when compiling
middleware for Vercel. This prevents middleware.js.nft.json from being
generated, causing the ENOENT build failure.

Fix: lightweight middleware that checks for Supabase session cookies
directly (no @supabase/ssr import). Full auth validation still happens
server-side via supabase.auth.getUser() on every protected page.

commit.message:
Add ORDER BY for deterministic query results (#12014)

* Add ORDER BY to StudyViewMapper ClickHouse queries for deterministic results

The getMutatedGenes, getCnaGenes, and getStructuralVariantGenes queries
in StudyViewMapper were missing ORDER BY clauses, causing non-deterministic
result ordering from ClickHouse. This led to flaky e2e screenshot diffs
where genes would appear in different positions across runs.

Adds ORDER BY totalCount DESC, hugo_gene_symbol ASC to match the
ClickhouseAlterationMapper queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ORDER BY to DiscreteCopyNumberMapper queries for deterministic results

The discrete copy number queries had no ORDER BY clause, making result
ordering dependent on the database engine internal behavior. Add
deterministic ordering by entrez_gene_id, alteration, and sample stable_id.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
